name: SDLC Assessment Approve Pipeline
description: Realistic software-delivery pipeline with one specialist assessment before release packaging.
outcome: An implemented change is assessed explicitly before release packaging continues.

definition.process_instructions:
Run the workflow through the authored stages `blueprint`, `implementation`, and `release-readiness`. Treat each stage as a distinct checkpoint of evidence and handoff quality: `blueprint` exists to Technical direction exists; `implementation` exists to The change is implemented and assessed; `release-readiness` exists to Release packaging is complete.
Use these roles deliberately: `approve-solution-architect` Produces implementation-ready technical direction; `approve-implementation-engineer` Implements scoped repository changes and tests them; `approve-acceptance-assessor` Assesses the implementation against the authored acceptance bar; `approve-release-coordinator` Packages release evidence after approved implementation.
Happy path: start with the earliest unfinished stage, dispatch the best matching producing role for that stage, require a full handoff with concrete artifacts or evidence, then move to the next stage only when the current output is usable. When a later stage is primarily evaluative, dispatch the reviewer or assessor roles after the producing role has handed off; when a later stage is packaging or release-oriented, dispatch the coordinating role only after upstream evidence is complete.
Unhappy path: If any role reports missing evidence, partial delivery, request changes, rejection, blocking risk, or an escalation, keep the current subject open, route work back to the most relevant producing role, and require a fresh handoff before trying again. If two decisions conflict, preserve both in history, but route using the newest subject state and the most restrictive still-active finding. Do not skip a stage just because a later role believes it can improvise around incomplete upstream work. This is the straight-through approval baseline. After `approve-implementation-engineer` delivers a full handoff, run `approve-acceptance-assessor`, and only then let `approve-release-coordinator` package the change.
Completion rules: finish only after the last required stage has a final, internally consistent handoff, the final package matches the current subject state, and every role named above has either contributed or been intentionally skipped for a clear reason grounded in the playbook. Do not fabricate approvals, assessments, escalations, or branch changes that the playbook does not actually justify, and do not treat stale findings as permission to advance new work.

parameters.name: goal
parameters.description: What this live workflow should accomplish

parameters.name: scenario_name
parameters.description: Stable scenario identifier that the CLI must echo in its assessment output

parameters.name: assessment_contract
parameters.description: Exact approval contract the acceptance assessor must enforce

columns.label: Planned
columns.description: Work is defined but not yet assigned.

columns.label: Blocked
columns.description: Work is blocked and needs intervention.

columns.label: Done
columns.description: The work item meets its acceptance criteria.

stages.name: implementation
stages.goal: The change is implemented and assessed.
